title: convolvulus cyclostegius var. intermedius
intro: Convolvulus cyclostegius var. intermedius, also known as the Intermediate Bindweed, is a plant species in the Convolvulaceae family, characterized by its climbing habit and trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are thought to aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Convolvulus cyclostegius var. intermedius include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of skin wounds, as well as its use as an antispasmodic to relieve muscle cramps and spasms. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are typically made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, and are used to harness its medicinal properties.
